source_meaning: To kill someone; to cause someone's death.

source_meaning: Used as an affirmation or accolade when one is deemed the top position in a comparison or contest; to win or be the best (at).

target_idiom: malhar enquanto o ferro está quente

source_meaning: To speak quickly or excessively.
target_meaning: to talk or speak excessively

source_meaning: A situation in which a person unsuccessfully attempts to contact another person by telephone and leaves a message instead, and in which the second person then unsuccessfully attempts to return the initial call and leaves a message for the first person, and so on as if the two are playing a game of tag in which the most recent person to have been left with a message is now designated as "it" (i.e. as the player now obliged to chase the other and to attempt anew to make contact).
target_meaning: cat-and-mouse game (situation where one party pursues another without securing a significant victory)

source_meaning: A major fuss over a trivial matter.
target_meaning: storm in a teacup (major fuss over a trivial matter)

source_meaning: Used with a comparative to express extreme heat or cold.
target_meaning: intensifier used with frio (“cold”) or calor (“heat”)

source_meaning: To be grateful, feel oneself fortunate, consider oneself lucky (especially in avoiding some calamity).
target_meaning: to be very lucky (usually used in the past tense)

source_meaning: An expression of disapproval or disbelief.
target_meaning: my ass! (indicates disapproval, disagreement, or disbelief, often with a tone of disregard, disdain, or disgust)

source_meaning: Gay; homosexual.
target_meaning: Said to announce that a man is gay or effeminate

source_meaning: said in reply to something that one believes will never happen
target_meaning: said of something one is confident that will not happen

source_idiom: the cat's out of the bag
source_meaning: It is too late. Particularly used of secrets that have been exposed.

target_idiom: com a pulga atrás da orelha
source_meaning: A general expression of distrust, particularly implying that another person is attempting to deceive the speaker, or that a situation is not, or can not be, as it appears.

source_meaning: Conclusive action has been taken, so events will proceed in an irreversible manner; the point of no return has been passed; the future is determined; there are no more options.

source_idiom: the shoe is on the other foot

source_meaning: Immensely, forcefully.

target_meaning: not to mention (used to introduce another important point)

source_meaning: used to add emphasis to "why" when beginning a question.
